evidence: In London, students mapped their ideal schools using paper and markers to prefigure cooperative spaces.
p1_justification: The practice focuses on mapping a material, affirmative alternative to the current alienated structure of schools.
p1_verbatim_evidence: mapped their ideal schools using paper and markers to prefigure cooperative spaces
p2_justification: Utilizes mapping as a creative, speculative exercise to model the future.

p3_items.justification: Cultivates active hope and forward-dreaming among children.

evidence: In New York, speculative workshops were conducted to defamiliarize standard learning technologies and investigate surveillance.
p1_justification: Explores warning scenarios of technological control while maintaining agency through writing.
p1_verbatim_evidence: defamiliarize standard learning technologies and investigate surveillance
p2_justification: Uses creative defamiliarization to prompt critical awareness of everyday technologies.
p2_verbatim_evidence: workshops were conducted to defamiliarize standard learning technologies

p3_items.justification: The warning system targets corporate data practices in higher education.

evidence: An online community co-created a Hopepunk-inspired curriculum for teachers worldwide to foster cooperative learning online.
p1_justification: Represents an active, community-driven effort to build a tangible alternative pedagogy.
p1_verbatim_evidence: co-created a Hopepunk-inspired curriculum for teachers worldwide

p3_items.justification: Expresses active, structured hope through community curriculum creation.
